<think>I should begin by stating the general legal drinking age in most places, while being clear that laws can vary.</think>
In many countries, including the United States, you must be at least 21 years old to purchase alcohol. 
<think>It's important to mention that this age requirement is not universal and can differ by country or region.</think>
However, the minimum legal age to buy alcohol varies worldwide, with some countries setting it at 18 or 19 years old. 
<think>I should add that even within countries, local laws or exceptions may apply.</think>
Some regions or states may have additional restrictions or exceptions based on local laws. 
<think>It would be helpful to advise the reader to check local regulations for precise information.</think>
Always check the specific laws in your area to ensure compliance with local alcohol purchase regulations.
